selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a clear understanding of the various types of fake content that exist. This includes misinformation, disinformation, and malicious content. Once you have identified the type of fake content you are dealing with, you can take appropriate actions to address it. For example, if it is misinformation, you can fact-check and correct it. If it is disinformation, you can debunk it by providing evidence to refute its claims. And if it is malicious content, you can report it to the platform or the authorities. It is also essential to have a system in place for regularly monitoring your social media accounts for any suspicious activity and taking swift action when necessary. Additionally, educating yourself and others on how to identify and deal with fake content can go a long way in preventing its spread.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must understand the different types of fake content, take appropriate actions based on their type, have a system for monitoring accounts, and educate oneself and others on how to identify and deal with fake content.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": []
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a clear understanding of the various types of fake content that exist. This includes misinformation, disinformation, and malicious content. Once you have identified the type of fake content you are dealing with, you can take appropriate actions to address it. For example, if it is misinformation, you can fact-check and correct it. If it is disinformation, you can debunk it by providing evidence to refute its claims. And if it is malicious content, you can report it to the platform or the authorities. It is also essential to have a system in place for regularly monitoring your social media accounts for any suspicious activity and taking swift action when necessary. Additionally, educating yourself and others on how to identify and deal with fake content can go a long way in preventing its spread.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must understand the different types of fake content, take appropriate actions based on their type, have a system for monitoring accounts, and educate oneself and others on how to identify and deal with fake content.
